2605.30295.title: MedCase-Structured: A Text-to-FHIR Dataset for Benchmarking Diagnostic Reasoning in Clinically Realistic EHR Settings
2605.30295.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) show promise for clinical reasoning and decision support, but evaluation in realistic, electronic health record-congruent settings remains limited. Existing benchmarks often rely on static datasets or unstructured inputs that do not reflect the structured, interoperable data formats used in clinical systems. We introduce a pipeline for generating clinically realistic HL7 FHIR R4 bundles from unstructured text, enabling controllable evaluation of clinical decision support systems. The pipeline combines staged LLM generation with terminology-grounded validation and repair to reduce hallucinated codes and enforce structural and semantic consistency. Applying this approach to MedCaseReasoning, we construct MedCase-Structured, a synthetic dataset aligned with clinician-authored diagnostic cases, achieving valid FHIR generation for 82.5% of cases. Evaluation on MedCase-Structured reveals consistently lower diagnostic accuracy for LLMs on structured FHIR inputs than with plain text, highlighting the importance of deployment-aligned benchmarking.
2605.30295.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）在臨床推理和決策支持方面顯示出潛力，但在現實的電子健康記錄相符環境中的評估仍然有限。現有的基準通常依賴於靜態數據集或不結構化的輸入，這些並不反映臨床系統中使用的結構化、可互操作的數據格式。我們介紹了一個從不結構化文本生成臨床現實的HL7 FHIR R4捆綁包的流程，使臨床決策支持系統的可控評估成為可能。該流程結合了分階段的LLM生成與基於術語的驗證和修復，以減少幻覺代碼並強制執行結構和語義的一致性。將這種方法應用於MedCaseReasoning，我們構建了MedCase-Structured，這是一個與臨床醫生撰寫的診斷案例對齊的合成數據集，實現了82.5%案例的有效FHIR生成。在MedCase-Structured上的評估顯示，LLMs在結構化FHIR輸入上的診斷準確性始終低於純文本，突顯了與部署對齊的基準測試的重要性。
2605.30295.author: Valentina Bui Muti et.al.
2605.30295.authors: Valentina Bui Muti, Eugénie Dulout, Ziquan Fu
